name: Innova DX 3-Disc Starter Set
beginnerNotes: The classic entry point. Includes Aviar, Shark, and Leopard.
description: Everything you need to start playing disc golf in one convenient package.

name: Aviar Putter (DX)
beginnerNotes: The most popular putter in disc golf. Very straight and predictable.
description: The Aviar Putt & Approach is the number one putter in disc golf.

name: Discraft Buzzz (Z Line)
beginnerNotes: Perfect for beginners. Holds whatever line you put it on.
description: The Buzzz is the most popular disc in the world. It's a super straight flying midrange.

name: Innova Leopard (Star)
beginnerNotes: Excellent first driver. Easy to throw straight with low power.
description: The Leopard should be everyone's first fairway driver.

name: Dynamic Discs Trooper Bag
beginnerNotes: Best value backpack. Holds 18+ discs and a large water bottle.
description: The Trooper is a great starter backpack for up to 18 discs.

name: UBeesize 67" Phone Tripod
beginnerNotes: Essential for recording your form and improving your throw.
description: A versatile tripod for recording your practice sessions.

name: Bushnell Edge Rangefinder
beginnerNotes: Takes the guesswork out of distance. Highly accurate.
description: The first rangefinder designed specifically for disc golf.

name: Adidas Terrex Swift R3 GTX
beginnerNotes: Waterproof and high-grip. The unofficial shoe of disc golf.
description: Lightweight, waterproof hiking shoes for all-day comfort.

name: Flight Towel Training Tool
beginnerNotes: Warm up your arm and practice your snap anywhere.
description: A unique training aid that uses a towel to simulate disc resistance.
